name: Hungary (Rákosi arms, 1949–1956)
description: Horizontal triband of red, white and green, charged in the centre of the white band with the Rákosi-era coat of arms — a circular emblem with a red five-pointed star above a stylised blue sky and rising sun, crossed hammer and ear of wheat bound with a red ribbon, all encircled by a wreath of wheat with a red-white-green ribbon at its base.
history: Adopted 20 August 1949 when the Stalinist Rákosi government promulgated a new constitution and coat of arms modelled on Soviet symbolism. During the 1956 Hungarian Revolution the arms were widely cut out of the flag, producing the iconic 'lyukas zászló'. The Rákosi arms were formally replaced by the Kádár arms on 23 May 1957.

features.symbolism: Soviet-style emblem — red star, sun and crossed hammer-and-wheat in a wheat wreath bound with a red-white-green ribbon
